broker_message: Hi Norman — I'm interested in 1673 East 18th #F9 but noticed it's shown 'in contract' and has cycled on and off the market. Is it actually available? If so, could you confirm the floor/walk-up, whether it's rent-stabilized (and the prior legal rent), full move-in costs, and whether the owner would consider $1,650 given how long it's been listed? Thanks.
broker_questions: Is F9 still available, or is the 7/1/2026 contract firm?
Why have prior contracts fallen through?
Which floor is F9, and is there an elevator?
Is 400 sqft the apartment interior only?
Is it rent-stabilized? Prior legal rent?
How reliable is heat/hot water in winter?
building_risk_rationale: Raw HPD count (10; augrented lists 11 'open' — open>total is inconsistent so exact open number unverified) is at/below the Sheepshead Bay avg of 16.1, but the pattern is the concern: recurring Heat/Hot Water complaints across 2024-2026 with an open Class C no-heat, plus above-average DOB (8 vs 2.1) and ECB (9 vs 0.9) and a 2/2024 bedbug filing. augrented model: 88% 12-month serious-failure risk. Medium, leaning high.
commute_notes: Kings Highway B/Q is close (~0.13 mi). Q express ~45-55 min to Midtown; B weekday express. Solid transit for deep-south Brooklyn.
cons: Shows 'in contract' with a history of failed deals — may not be available
Recurring building heat/hot-water complaints incl. open Class C
Very small 400 sqft footprint, floor/walk-up status undisclosed
leverage_notes: 37 days on market with repeated failed contracts and a small footprint give leverage; comps span $1,649-$1,850, so $1,750 is mid-range with room to push toward the low end.
most_likely_sitting_reason: Repeated contracts falling through (churn) on a small studio, plus a building with recurring maintenance complaints
noise_risks: Kings Highway is a busy commercial corridor one block away
Older 1940 building — inter-unit and street noise possible
plain_english: A small, no-fee renovated studio a short walk from the Kings Highway B/Q — decent transit and a fair mid-range price. Catches: it keeps going into contract and coming back, it was 'in contract' when checked, and the building has a recurring winter heat/hot-water problem incl. an open immediately-hazardous violation. Only pursue if the broker confirms it's actually available, gives the floor, and you can negotiate toward ~$1,650. Confirm rent-stabilization and prior legal rent.
pros: No broker fee and close to Kings Highway B/Q (~0.13 mi)
Priced mid-range vs area studio comps with room to negotiate
Renovated kitchen/bath and hardwood floors per listing

red_flags.how_to_verify: Ask directly whether F9 is still available before touring
red_flags.why: May already be spoken for; could be a lead-harvesting listing

red_flags.how_to_verify: Measure on tour; confirm whether 400 SF is the apartment only
red_flags.issue: Tiny 400 sqft studio described as '2 rooms' with 12x17 LR and 10x12 kitchen
red_flags.why: Cited room dimensions barely fit in 400 sqft, leaving little for bath/closets

red_flags.how_to_verify: Ask which floor F9 is on and whether there is an elevator
red_flags.issue: Floor not disclosed in a walk-up building
red_flags.why: If upper floor, a walk-up burden with no elevator noted

red_flags.how_to_verify: Ask super and current/former tenants; check HPD Online
red_flags.why: Multiple 311/HPD heat complaints incl. open Class C

sitting_reasons.ask_before_applying: Ask why prior applicants backed out and whether the 7/1 contract is firm
sitting_reasons.evidence: Price history shows a cycle: listed 6/15, in-contract 6/16, relisted, and again 'In contract 7/1/2026' at a flat $1,750
sitting_reasons.reason: Repeated in-contract / relist churn (deals falling through)

sitting_reasons.ask_before_applying: Ask about winter heat reliability and the unit's floor / walk-up status
sitting_reasons.evidence: augrented shows recurring Heat/Hot Water 311/HPD across 2024-2026 incl. an open Class C no-heat item
sitting_reasons.reason: 400 sqft studio in a 1940 walk-up with recurring building heat/hot-water complaints

unverified: Exact live HPD open count (11 open vs 10 total — inconsistent)
Whether unit F9 is genuinely available or in contract
Rent-stabilized status and prior legal rent
Exact move-in costs and lease term
